host: DGX Spark (GB10, aarch64), local Ollama at http://localhost:11434/v1
model: gpt-oss:120b (agent); deterministic EVTX fallback produced the sealed verdict

verdict.note: INDETERMINATE is the policy default for single-source EVTX regardless of what was found; a matching verdict is NOT evidence that coverage was complete.

unread_file_contents_verified_out_of_ban.parser: python-evtx via uv on the Spark
unread_file_contents_verified_out_of_ban.meaning: the entire lateral-movement half of the case (logons + remote WMI process execution) was present but never ingested

scorecard.name: Valid Accounts (network logon)
scorecard.detail: File never parsed. Manifest lists T1078 as covered_no_finding purely via generic evtx_query->technique attribution, NOT from any actual 4624 record. Spurious coverage credit.

scorecard.name: Windows Management Instrumentation (remote execution)
scorecard.detail: Double miss: the file was never parsed AND T1047 is not one of the coverage manifest's 12 ATT&CK targets, so the remote-WMI signature could not earn coverage even if read.

scorecard.name: Create/Modify System Process: Windows Service (persistence)
scorecard.detail: 1 finding f-B-evtx-service-install, confidence HYPOTHESIS, mapped to T1543.003, timeline event emitted.

scorecard.name: SpoolFool - Print Spooler privilege escalation
scorecard.detail: Recognized in narrative: attack_story carries cves:[CVE-2022-21999], named_technique 'SpoolFool', and a concrete hunt query. But it is scored ONLY as T1543.003 persistence; the privilege-escalation dimension (e.g. T1068) and the CVE are not elevated into observed_techniques or scored coverage.

root_causes: RC1 (primary) - single-file evidence scoping: the case is a directory with 2 EVTX files but evtx_path / image_path / evidence_path all resolved to one file; the WMI file was never enumerated, zeroing out the lateral-movement half of the case.
RC2 - coverage manifest lacks T1047: the WmiPrvSE.exe-parent remote-WMI signature is not among the 12 scored ATT&CK targets, so it earns no coverage credit even when present.

RC3 - thin tool battery on the deterministic fallback: only evtx_query ran (2 tool_calls incl. case_open); hayabusa_scan / Sigma never fired, so no rule-based detection of WMI lateral movement or SpoolFool.
RC4 - SpoolFool technique-mapping: the named CVE signature is recognized in prose but mapped only to persistence (T1543.003), not to its privilege-escalation technique.
remediation_proposals_not_yet_implemente: Fallback evidence scoping should enumerate every file in a case directory and run evtx_query per file (or a merged query), not select one.
Add T1047 and a WmiPrvSE.exe-parent heuristic to the coverage manifest targets.
Run hayabusa_scan / Sigma on the fallback path when the binary is available.
Map named CVE signatures (SpoolFool) to their priv-esc technique (T1068) in addition to the service-install persistence artifact.
